fed_monetary: The FOMC has held the federal funds rate at 3.50–3.75% for a second consecutive meeting, with Chair Powell acknowledging that tariffs and the Iran-driven energy shock will likely produce 'higher inflation and slower growth,' while emphasizing the Fed can 'stay patient.' With core PCE at 3.06% (vs. 2.8% estimate), core CPI at 2.73%, and PPI running at 3.22% — all above consensus — the policy path remains on hold; market pricing for a 2026 cut is now coalescing around a single 25bp reduction at best, with the Cleveland Fed's 1-year inflation expectation anchored near 2.29% providing thin cover for easing.
labor_data: The March employment report delivered a decisive beat, with NFP adding 178K jobs against a 100K consensus estimate, while the unemployment rate held in-line at 4.3%. Weekly initial claims of 202K (vs. 210K estimate) reinforce that the labor market remains resilient despite tightening financial conditions. The combination of above-trend job creation and sticky wages sustains household income support, but also limits the Fed's scope to pivot dovishly in the near term.
fiscal_supply: The CBO projects the FY2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion, with federal debt on a trajectory toward 120% of GDP by 2036, sustaining elevated Treasury net issuance. A 30-year bond auction is scheduled for April 9, and a full refunding calendar through mid-April adds supply headwind to the long end; the 10Y yield at 4.34% remains technically elevated relative to the 4.0% threshold, and the TGA balance of $847.7B provides the Treasury modest tactical flexibility. Liquidity conditions remain below-average, with the composite liquidity score at the 31st percentile.
corporate: S&P 500 Q1 2026 earnings are projected to grow 13.2% year-over-year — the sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit growth — with aggregate estimated earnings of $629.3B, up modestly since December. However, HY OAS at 312bp and IG OAS at 108bp reflect contained but watchful credit markets, as WTI crude at $95.48/bbl sustains input cost pressure and the SPX's forward price-to-FCF ratio at 27.4x sits 37% above its 20-year average. VIX at 21.26 — above the 20 threshold — signals residual investor anxiety heading into Q1 reporting season.
next_catalyst: The March CPI report, due April 10, is the single most consequential near-term release: consensus forecasts a jump to approximately 3.1–3.4% YoY headline (from 2.66% in February), driven by an estimated 11% surge in the energy component linked to the Strait of Hormuz disruption — a print at or above the upper bound would materially reset Fed easing expectations for 2026.
sources: Fed holds rates at 3.50–3.75%, Powell warns tariffs could cause 'higher inflation and slower growth' — Federal Reserve Board / CBS News
March NFP adds 178K jobs, smashing 100K estimate; unemployment holds at 4.3% — U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, April 3, 2026
March CPI preview: headline seen jumping to 3.1–3.4% YoY on 11% energy spike; report due April 10 — FactSet / J.P. Morgan via Morningstar
Brent crude surpassed $100/bbl on March 8 after Strait of Hormuz closure; Brent peaked at $126/bbl; WTI now $95.48 — 2026 Strait of Hormuz Crisis / Dallas Fed
CBO projects FY2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion; federal debt to reach 120% of GDP by 2036 — Congressional Budget Office
